title: ForgeBrain Lesson Blueprint
description: The structured, single-concept lesson plan produced by the lesson stage from a research brief. Produced after research (see research-spec.md) and consumed by the Script Generator. See lesson-spec.md for the full process this schema supports.

topic_id.description: Matches the corresponding id in curriculum/java-roadmap.json and the topic_id of the research brief this lesson was built from.

topic_title.description: Matches the corresponding title in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

lesson_objective.description: The one concept this lesson teaches. Everything else in the blueprint must serve this single objective — see lesson-spec.md Section 4.

lesson_summary.description: 2-3 sentences on what the lesson covers and how it approaches it. A quick-scan overview before the step-by-step detail.

key_points.description: 3-5 ordered points that build toward lesson_objective. Not a list of separate concepts — every point must serve the same objective.

step_by_step_explanation.description: The ordered teaching beats, 3-6 recommended. The walkthrough the Script Generator turns into narration, beat by beat.

analogy.description: The one analogy this lesson uses, carried or refined from the research brief's simple_analogy.

common_mistakes.description: 1-3 items. The first entry is the lesson's one memorable contrast or mistake; any additional entries are secondary/reference material, not necessarily spoken.

what_to_avoid_saying.description: Carried directly from the research brief's safety_notes. Statements that must not appear anywhere in the resulting script.

beginner_takeaway.description: The one sentence a viewer should walk away remembering.

retention_hook.description: A committed opening line or hook concept for this specific lesson's angle, sharper than the research brief's general topic_summary.

visual_notes.description: 1-3 short visual cue descriptions. The first entry is the lesson's one required visual cue; any additional entries are secondary/optional.

audience_level.description: Carried forward from the research brief unless explicitly overridden for this lesson.

target_duration_seconds.description: Carried forward from the research brief's target_reel_length_seconds unless overridden. Bounds key_points and step_by_step_explanation length.

teaching_style.description: The approach this lesson takes to its one concept. See lesson-spec.md Section 8 for how new styles get added.

lesson_version.description: Version of the lesson process/schema that produced this blueprint, for future caching and migration.

generated_at.description: When this lesson blueprint was produced.

based_on_research_version.description: The research_version of the research brief this lesson was built from, for traceability.

coreExample.description: The one chosen example for this lesson. Not final on-screen code — the Script Generator formats and times it.

description.description: What the example demonstrates and why it was chosen over the other code_example_ideas the research brief offered.

code_sketch.description: A short, illustrative Java code sketch. Not production-formatted code — a rough shape for the Script Generator to refine.

focus_note.description: What part of the example should be emphasized on screen (e.g. a specific line), so the storyboard knows what to highlight.

overall_confidence.description: Coarse self-assessment of how well-grounded this lesson's narrowing choices are.

flagged_uncertainties.description: Judgment calls the lesson stage had to make (e.g. narrowing between multiple strong research concepts), flagged for reviewer attention.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Reserved for future multi-source or multi-version contradiction handling. Empty until relevant.
